name: Canggu Sandbar (峇里島 - 沙洲浪點 / The Sandbar / Pantai Sandbar Canggu)
gps_location: -8.6450, 115.1240 (approx.; located between Echo Beach and Pererenan Rivermouth, in front of La Brisa / Sand Bar Echo Beach) [uncertain]
distance_from_dps_airport_km: 23 km (approx. 75-90 min by car depending on traffic)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu (Batu Bolong center): 5-10 min by scooter / paddle; From Uluwatu: 75-100 min by scooter
wave_type: Beach break with reef-like behavior - A-frame peak with strong left-hand wedge (longer ride) and shorter punchy right; produces steep takeoffs, fast hollow barrels and ripable walls over sand bottom
skill_level: Advanced to Pro (intermediate-advanced on smaller days; expert only over head-and-a-half)
wave_height_range: 3-10+ ft (best 4-8 ft; holds up to double-overhead - the largest swell-tolerant break of the Echo Beach zone)
bottom_type: Sand (sand bar formed at the rivermouth/Echo zone; some submerged rocks at lower tides on the edges)
best_season: Dry season May-October prime (SW groundswells + offshore SE/E trade winds). April and November are good shoulder months. Year-round wave but wet season (Dec-Feb) often onshore and murky.
best_tide: High tide (longest ride and connection to the inside section). Works mid-tide with shorter wedgy peaks; low tide exposes submerged rocks and shortens the wave.
best_wind: Offshore from East / Southeast (E-SE trade winds); turns onshore/side-shore from late morning onwards
best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also S and SSW
swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 (consistent during dry season; needs slightly more size than Batu Bolong to switch on properly)
wave_length_meters: 40-100 m (medium-length ride; longer when the left connects to the inside section on high tide) [uncertain]
wave_speed_rating: Fast (steep, punchy, wedgy takeoff; rewards shortboards and step-ups on bigger days)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Difficult - steep ledgy A-frame takeoff that jacks up quickly into a heavy thick-lipped barrel; sand bottom is forgiving relative to reef but the drop is critical and unpredictable
secondary_peaks: A-frame splits into a longer left (the marquee wave) and a shorter right shoulder; an inside reform/section can be connected at high tide; River Mouth peak sits immediately to the north and Echo Left to the south
peak_crowd_level: 3/5 - significantly less crowded than Echo Left or Batu Bolong because of the paddle/skill barrier; gets busy when good but the take-off zone is more spread out. Peak crowd 7-10am dry season.
access: Walk-in from the beach at Echo Beach / La Brisa area (end of Jl. Pura Batu Mejan), then a moderate paddle north along the inside toward the sand bar; alternatively, walk from the Pererenan side and paddle south. No boat charter required.
paddle_out_method: Use the channel directly in front of the Sandbar peak to paddle out, watching for submerged rocks at lower tides. There is no convenient clean channel like at beginner spots, so timing sets is important. A useful rip on the south side of the peak can help push you out.
entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking at Echo Beach lots: IDR 2,000-5,000 for scooter, IDR 5,000-10,000 for car.
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for glassy offshores and lighter crowd; second session at sunset if wind drops.
best_time_of_day: Before 8am to get clean offshore conditions and avoid both the build-up of crowd and the onshore wind that arrives mid-morning.
nearby_warung: Sand Bar (beachfront bar named after the wave), La Brisa, The Lawn, Echo Beach House, Beach House, Mocca Cafe; warung and coconut stalls along the Echo Beach access road.
alternative_spot_within_10min: Echo Left (right next door - heavier reef alternative), River Mouth (immediately north), Pererenan (5 min north - reef option), Batu Bolong / Old Man's (5-10 min south for smaller mellower waves), Berawa (10 min south).
boat_charter_required: No - paddle-in from the beach. Some surfers paddle from Echo or Pererenan; boat access not standard.
hazards: Powerful thick-lipped wave that breaks heavy; strong rip currents around the peak and on the paddle-out; submerged rocks at low tide; wedgy unpredictable closeouts; occasional crowd-related collisions; river-mouth bacterial water quality after rain (Yeh Poh runoff just north)
marine_hazards: Minimal reef life since bottom is mostly sand; possible jellyfish in wet season (transparent box-style stings rare); rare stingrays in shallows - shuffle feet; bacterial water issues post-rain due to nearby river-mouth runoff. No significant shark concern.
reef_cut_severity_index: Low to Medium - bottom is predominantly sand so reef cuts are uncommon; submerged rocks at low tide on the inside can cause bruises/cuts but nothing like Echo Left reef
rip_current_pattern: Strong rip running through the channel adjacent to the Sandbar peak (used for paddle-out but can drag you out fast); side-sweeping current along the beach toward River Mouth on outgoing tide; rip strength increases significantly on bigger swells
lineup_etiquette: Priority on the peak; rotation is observed but more relaxed than Echo Left; don't drop in on the deeper surfer; greet/nod to locals and regulars; respect Indonesian surfers who have priority
localism_intensity: Low to Medium - less locally policed than Echo Left because the crowd is more transient (advanced visitors and bodyboarders); aggression rare if etiquette is respected
respect_protocol: Earn your place by sitting wide initially; don't paddle inside on the peak; if you're a strong intermediate, surf Echo Right or Berawa first; smile and say hi to the regulars
resident_local_legends: Canggu free-surf and bodyboard crew shred the Sandbar regularly; international travelling pros pass through during big swells; specific named legends not consistently documented [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Pura Batu Mejan (Hindu temple) sits behind Echo Beach - respect ceremonies and offerings; observe Nyepi (Day of Silence, March) - no surfing, beach closed; women on menstruation traditionally avoid temple grounds; cover up walking through the village.
medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major trauma), Siloam Hospital Denpasar.
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~2-3 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~27 km; Siloam Denpasar ~17 km
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski stationed at the Sandbar; Balawista lifeguards informally cover Echo Beach area on busy days; no formal water-patrol
emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
nearest_surf_school: Most schools (Dian Surf School, Pro Surf, Sea N Surf, Surf Well Bali, Odyssey) operate from Batu Bolong and rarely teach at Sandbar due to wave power; intermediate-advanced surf guiding offered by Padang Padang Surf Camp and Mondo Surf Village.
board_rental_availability: No on-beach rental at the Sandbar peak. Rental shops on Echo Beach road (Boardriders Bali Echo Beach, Drifter, Deus Ex Machina) offer shortboards and step-ups suited to Sandbar.
surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land photographers shoot the Sandbar peak from the beach and adjacent rocks during dry season; photos sold via Instagram or QR code same day
photographer_recommendation: Local Canggu/Echo photographers (freelancers via @balisurfphotos and similar); typical price IDR 200,000-400,000 for an edited photo pack [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Echo Beach House, Mocca Bali, Hotel Tugu Bali (luxury heritage), Pondok Nyoman, surf camps such as Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu and Mondo Surf Village; full spectrum from hostels to villas within 5-15 min.
webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d40 (Surfline Canggu cam covers Echo/Sandbar area); https://balicams.com/camera/rivermouth (River Mouth cam adjacent)
drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for surf photography (DGCA registration required for >250g). Avoid flying over the temple, directly over the crowded lineup, or during low-altitude takeoffs. Many photographers fly drones here daily.
coworking_nearby: Yes - Dojo Bali, Outpost Canggu, B Work Bali (Nelayan), Tropical Nomad; all within 5-15 min scooter.
avg_lesson_price: IDR 500,000-800,000 (~USD 32-50 / TWD 1,000-1,600) for intermediate guiding (most schools do not teach beginners here)
board_rental_daily: Performance shortboard IDR 150,000-200,000/day; step-up IDR 175,000-250,000/day from nearby Echo Beach rental shops
water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Fair to Good. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to Yeh Poh river-mouth runoff and Echo Beach drainage; ear/skin infection risk. Avoid surfing 24-48h after heavy rain.
annual_competition_window: No dedicated WSL or major event at the Sandbar; occasional free-surf sessions and informal contests piggyback off Echo Beach events [uncertain]
community_events: Sunset sessions at La Brisa and Sand Bar; Trash Hero Canggu and 4Ocean beach clean-ups; Deus 9ft & Single Festival activities nearby in Canggu; full-moon beach gatherings.
